steps.title: Identify the claim that needs support
steps.content:
The passage calls steady energy loads “an absolute prerequisite for wireless communication devices to be powered piezoelectrically.” Supporting evidence must directly show why wireless devices need steady energy — i.e., that unsteady energy causes them to fail or underperform.
  • "The near-constant kinetic vibration of a spacecraft makes it possible to power its wireless communication devices using only non-CFRP piezoelectric harvesters.": Describes a supply source (spacecraft vibration) and a harvester type, but says nothing about whether steadiness of supply is required for wireless devices to function. ✗
  • "The high conductivity of the CFRP makes the piezoelectric harvester sufficient for wireless communication devices.": Explains how the CFRP design works, not why steady energy is a prerequisite — it skips the premise and goes to the solution. ✗
  • "Intermittent or unpredictable electrical supply undermines the efficacy of wireless communication devices.": If unreliable power degrades wireless devices, then steady power is logically required — directly proving the passage’s “absolute prerequisite” claim. ✓
  • "The CFRP electrode is incompatible with most wireless communication devices.": Contradicts the passage, which presents the CFRP harvester as a viable solution for powering wireless devices. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The claim is that steady energy is an absolute prerequisite. Showing that unsteady energy undermines wireless device efficacy is the logical proof of that prerequisite. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
